channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SpaceX launches U.S and Japanese landers to the moon
item.description: In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jump-start business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco offers AI application visibility, access control, threat defense
item.description: Cisco has rolled out a service that promises to protect enterprise AI development projects with visibility, access control, threat defense, and other safeguards.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Reimagine application modernisation with the power of generative AI
item.description: In a global economy where innovators increasingly win big, too many enterprises are stymied by legacy application systems. As a consequence, these businesses experience increased operational costs and find it difficult to scale or integrate modern technologies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A new movement wants to protect social media from billionaires
item.description: Over the past few years, concerns about billionaires using media outlets they own to signal boost their own beliefs has become a growing concern. Jeff Bezos owns the Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US Semiconductor Law Faces Criticism Over Cost and Effectiveness
item.description: A new report by the Peterson Institute for International Economics questions the effectiveness of a 2022 law aimed at increasing US semiconductor production, citing high costs and uncertain benefits.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At CES, PC makers aim for business, highlight AI-ready hardware
item.description: Enterprise computers are often the ugly ducklings of the PC world, viewed as dull, slow and less feature-rich than their consumer counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ericsson unveils genAI assistant for 5G network operations
item.description: Telecommunications and networking provider Ericsson recently launched its generative AI-based virtual assistant that uses large language model (LLM) technology to read, understand, and generate new content to provide personalized answers for network operators configuring wireless 5G networks, troubl
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications
item.description: The Homeland Security Department's Science and Technology Directorate has an ongoing research program for the development of cellular. The post Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers claim massive breach of company that tracks and sells Americans' location data
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals that hackers stole data from Gravy Analytics, a major seller of smartphone location data to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First 15 things to do or try first when you get a new iPhone
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson: If you just bought a new iPhone, here’s what to do first when you fire it up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Next-gen Ethernet standards set to move forward in 2025
item.description: 2024 was a banner year for Ethernet, which has been a foundational element of networking for more than 50 years and is poised for more advancements as 2025 kicks off. Multiple standards and certification efforts made progress in 2024, helping networks to be more reliable and deliver more bandwidth.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Opinion: The case for killing the tax credit for electric vehicles
item.description: In today's mature EV market, such credits provide tax cuts for the rich and discourage other green technologies.
item.dc:creator: Veronique de Rugy, The Mercury News

item.title: For Microsoft, 2025 could be a game-changing year
item.description: It’s looking like 2025 will be one of the most consequential years for Microsoft in a long time. Since Satya Nadella became CEO in early 2014, the company has been on an upward trajectory, despite a few bumps along the way.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The cloud cost wake-up call I predicted
item.description: For years, I’ve cautioned organizations about the hidden downsides of cloud computing. Ironically, I’m “Dave the cloud guy” who warns enterprises about the cloud. The benefits of the cloud’s agility, scalability, and innovation can quickly become a financial sinkhole without proper management.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CISA publishes security goals for software development process, product design
item.description: The US Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has published IT sector-specific goals (IT SSGs) to protect against cyber threats, including 11 software development process goals and seven product design goals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The devops certifications tech companies want
item.description: Devops continues to expand in development environments everywhere from small startups to the largest global enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: PowerSchool data breach exposes millions of student and teacher records
item.description: PowerSchool, which serves over 60 million students and teachers globally, has become the latest target of cybercriminals. Millions of records were stolen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why JavaScript’s still on top in 2025
item.description: Welcome to a new year of programming and the brand new monthly list of JavaScript stories just for developers! Among the highlights so far: Svelte and SvelteKit have seen a slew of incremental improvements, Astro.js 5.0 just hit, and the Next.js team is working on a composable caching mechanism.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Solar device transforms used tires to help purify water so that it's drinkable
item.description: Scientists have developed a floating solar still device that is capable of harnessing the sun's energy to purify seawater and withstanding harsh maritime conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World
